title: Live coverage: SpaceX to launch 24 Starlink satellites on Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg SFB
summary: The Starlink 17-39 mission is SpaceX’s 85th Falcon 9 rocket launch of the year. Liftoff from pad 4E at Vandenberg Space Force Base is scheduled for 7:36 a.m. PDT (10:36 a.m. EDT / 1436 UTC).

title: Plato's electronics ready for space
summary: The European Space Agency’s terrestrial planet hunter Plato has recently completed a series of key tests inside the Maxwell Test Chamber at ESTEC, demonstrating that its electronic equipment is fit to work in space.

title: AI rocket engine startup LegendSpace raises $29.5 million angel round
summary:

title: Artemis II crew at ESTEC
summary: The four Artemis II astronauts visited ESTEC, ESA’s technical centre in the Netherlands, on 13 July as the first stop in a series of post-flight visits to the European teams that made their mission possible.

title: From system to signal at ESA’s Navigation Training Course
summary: From 22-26 June, 30 university students from Europe and Canada came together at the European Space Agency (ESA) Academy’s Training and Learning Facility in Belgium to explore the fundamentals of global navigation satellite systems (GNSS). The course prepared the next generation of navigation professionals with lectures from experts and hands-on exercises where students applied their new knowledge.

title: Shaping the Emerald City
summary: The hills and basins of Seattle, Washington, have been sculpted by glaciers and modified by humans.

title: Mauritius becomes 70th nation to sign the Artemis Accords
summary:
The island nation of Mauritius signed the Artemis Accords July 17, becoming the 70th nation to do so in less than six years.
The post Mauritius becomes 70th nation to sign the Artemis Accords appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: Space Force triples launch contract ceiling amid rising demand
summary:
The maximum value of the National Security Space Launch Phase 3 Lane 1 contract was raised from $5.6 billion to $17 billion
